title: sebastiania bidentata var. odontococca
intro: Sebastiania bidentata var. odontococca, also known as Sabadilla, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel parasites and insects, due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Sabadilla is often used in the form of tincture, infusion, or ointment, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
